patterns.comment: cljgo builtin forms (ADR 0009/0011/0014): highlighted before delegating to the Clojure grammar
patterns.name: keyword.control.cljgo

patterns.comment: core.async control forms (ADR 0040): go bodies are real goroutines
patterns.name: keyword.control.async.cljgo

patterns.comment: core.async channel/buffer/pipeline surface (ADR 0040). Excludes map/merge/reduce/take/into/transduce — those async names shadow clojure.core and the Clojure grammar already colors them.
patterns.name: support.function.async.cljgo

patterns.comment: Result/Option track (ADR 0014). `some` stays Clojure's — the Option side is just/none (precedence principle).
patterns.name: support.function.result.cljgo

patterns.comment: `none` is a value, not a call (ADR 0014)
patterns.name: constant.language.result.cljgo

patterns.comment: Reader-conditional selectors (ADR 0036/0050). cljgo is its own platform: its feature is :cljgo, never :clj.
patterns.name: keyword.control.readcond.cljgo

patterns.comment: Everything else is stock Clojure — cljgo adds zero new syntax
